entries.original_incomplete_question: The figure below shows a grid of [BLANK] squares in a row. Each square has a diagonal connecting its lower left vertex to its upper right vertex. A bug moves along the line segments from vertex to vertex, never traversing the same segment twice and never moving from right to left along a horizontal or diagonal segment. Let $N$ be the number of paths the bug can take from the lower left corner ($A$) to the upper right corner ($B$). One such path from $A$ to $B$ is shown by the thick line segments in the figure. Find \sqrt{N}.

variants.variant: The diagram below depicts a row of [BLANK] squares arranged in a grid. Each square features a diagonal that connects its bottom left corner to its top right corner. A bug travels along the line segments from one vertex to another, avoiding any repeated traversal of the same segment and refraining from moving leftward along a horizontal or diagonal segment. Let $N$ represent the total number of distinct paths available for the bug to journey from the bottom left corner ($A$) to the top right corner ($B$). A particular path from $A$ to $B$ is illustrated by the bold line segments in the diagram. Calculate \sqrt{N}.

The illustration below depicts a grid of [BLANK] squares arranged in a single row. Each square features a diagonal that connects its lower left corner to its upper right corner. A bug travels along the line segments from one vertex to another, avoiding any segment it has already crossed and refraining from moving from right to left along either horizontal or diagonal segments. Let $N$ represent the total number of paths available for the bug to travel from the lower left corner ($A$) to the upper right corner ($B$). A particular path from $A$ to $B$ is indicated by the bold line segments in the illustration. Determine \sqrt{N}.

. The figure below shows a grid of [BLANK] squares in a row. Each square has a diagonal connecting its lower left vertex to its upper right vertex. The illustration below depicts a layout of [BLANK] squares arranged in a single row. Each square features a diagonal line that connects its bottom left corner to its top right corner. A bug navigates along the line segments from one vertex to another, without repeating any segment and without moving leftward along any horizontal or diagonal line. Let $N$ represent the total number of possible routes the bug can take from the bottom left corner ($A$) to the top right corner ($B$). A specific route from $A$ to $B$ is represented by the bold line segments in the illustration. Calculate \sqrt{N}.

The illustration below depicts a grid consisting of [BLANK] squares aligned in a single row. Each square features a diagonal that connects its bottom left corner to its top right corner. A bug travels along the line segments between vertices, avoiding the traversal of any segment more than once and refraining from moving leftward along any horizontal or diagonal segment. Let $N$ represent the total number of unique paths the bug can take from the bottom left corner ($A$) to the top right corner ($B$). A specific path from $A$ to $B$ is indicated by the bold line segments in the illustration. Calculate \sqrt{N}.
